title: Service Deep Dive
module_title: Multi-Service Enumeration and Attack Path Identification
prerequisites: SMB, SNMP, DNS, NFS enumeration tools
learning_objectives: Execute comprehensive enumeration of diverse network services
Cross-reference findings from multiple services to identify attack paths
Prioritize vulnerabilities based on exploitability and impact
key_topics: SNMP community string testing and walks
DNS zone transfer attempts and brute-forcing
scenario: 5 hosts running SMB, SNMP, DNS, NFS, and a web application. Ports are open — enumerate each service thoroughly and identify attack paths.

scenario_context.setting: Internal network segment with multiple services on diverse hosts
scenario_context.challenge: Correlate findings across services to identify exploitation sequences
scenario_context.relevance: Realistic internal pentest scenario common in enterprise networks

subtasks.title: SMB Enumeration
subtasks.description: Extract share listings, user accounts, password policies, OS versions.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use enum4linux, impacket tools, and rpcclient for comprehensive SMB enumeration. Extract share listings, identify null sessions, enumerate users and groups. Document OS and patch levels.
subtasks.evidence_required: Complete SMB enumeration output with user and share inventory

subtasks.title: SNMP Harvesting
subtasks.description: Test default community strings. Full SNMP walks.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Attempt common community strings (public, private, etc.). Conduct full SNMP walks on successful connections. Extract system info, ARP tables, routing tables.
subtasks.evidence_required: SNMP walk results with intelligence summary extracted

subtasks.title: DNS Intelligence
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Try zone transfers using dig and nslookup. Conduct subdomain brute-forcing if zone transfer fails. Map internal DNS records and document infrastructure.
subtasks.evidence_required: DNS enumeration results + internal network topology map

subtasks.title: NFS & Additional Services
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use showmount to enumerate NFS exports. Test mount access. Enumerate RPC services with rpcinfo. Document accessible shares and available services.
subtasks.evidence_required: NFS/RPC enumeration results with data inventory

subtasks.title: Consolidated Report
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create comprehensive enumeration report combining all service findings. Identify relationships between systems. Highlight critical information.

subtasks.title: Attack Path Identification
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Analyse cross-service dependencies. Identify 3 most promising exploitation sequences. Justify selection based on feasibility and impact. Create attack chain diagrams.
subtasks.evidence_required: Attack path analysis document with visual chains

reflection_prompt: How did findings from one service inform your approach to another? What hidden dependencies did you discover between systems?

quiz.question: What is the primary value of SNMP community string enumeration in a pentest?
options: Device management and network topology discovery
quiz.explanation: SNMP provides network topology, device inventory, and routing information, which is critical for understanding network structure and identifying targets.

quiz.question: How can an NFS share enumeration lead to lateral movement?
options: Mounted shares can contain SSH keys or credential files
quiz.explanation: Accessible NFS shares may contain home directories, SSH keys, and credential files that enable lateral movement to other systems.

portfolio_artifacts: NFS share inventory and mount attempts
mentor_notes: Evaluate depth of enumeration per service and quality of cross-service correlation. The attack paths should be technically sound and justified. Time management is critical with 48-hour deadline.
